All right, I want to look at another website for a client that we help. This is Liberty ID. Liberty ID sells something that works a little bit like an insurance package against identity theft. It's more than insurance. If you get your identity stolen and somebody ruins your life, their army of people go in and help you get your life back. They help you replace all your credit cards. They help you improve your credit rating if it got ruined. They help you get all the money back that was stolen. So it normally takes hundreds of hours on the phone to get your life back. They actually get on the phone for you and they can take hundreds of hours on the phone trying to get your life back into three hours on the phone with one of their representatives. And it's a short, it's a little bit of money a month, $15 or $20 a month in order to get their service. One of the problems that Liberty had is they were comparing themselves to competition. But the competition wasn't really doing what they do. The competition was trying to keep people from having their identity stolen, which Liberty argued doesn't happen. You can't really keep it from happening. And so they were selling something that is ultimately useless. And Liberty is saying, look, we're different. When it happens, because it's probably going to happen, we are going to get everything back for you. So it's a little bit of a different product. And I encourage them, hey, don't try to compete with these guys. Define a different space in the market. And that really changed a lot of things. The other thing is we realized that the closest product to what they were offering in terms of us understanding what it was was really insurance. But it's not called insurance. And there are different reasons they can't call it insurance. But we used the direct call to action of get covered to make it feel like an insurance product, which is mostly, it's pretty much exactly what it is, but they can't call it that because of some sort of regulation. But let me show you their final website. I think they did a fantastic job. Their business is increasing. But let me show you the website that they came up with after we wire framed a website for them. You see Liberty ID, get covered for identity theft. This is very different than the messaging that they used to use. And so now we know what they offer. They offer coverage. You can download a free report. You can get pricing or the obvious call to action. Again, you want an obvious call to action for your customers. The direct call to action, get covered. Then you have an ominous guy. They really went sort of negative. Like bad things can happen. I think it's an effective use of a picture here. When identity theft happens to you, notice we didn't say if. We say when. We get everything back. Now I know exactly what's in it for me. Then I can get covered here. And we went with a cost. They steal your time. They destroy your credit. They ruin your life. Now look, this is what they get for me. And this is the pain point. They've got to agitate a pain here in order to get me to get covered. So I'm not going to feel this pain. They need to agitate this pain because I haven't been robbed yet. But we know it's going to happen. By the way, I bought the product for me and my entire staff after understanding the statistics. It's a great product. Then are you covered? We wanted to continue to agitate this pain. And it's almost like Ray's sales letter, where he said, present a problem, then amplify the problem. So we've presented the problem here. We're amplifying it here. Are you covered? You have auto insurance, home insurance, health insurance, life insurance. You don't have identity theft insurance. And so we need to check this one off, too. Great use of visual on a website. Try to get creative like this as your wire framing websites for your company or for your clients. We have helped over 60,000 people get their lives back. That's the authority. That's you can trust us after their identities have been stolen. Then the plan. Get covered, contact us, get everything back. Buy the coverage, contact us when something happens, and we will get everything back and then get covered right here. But notice, one, two, three. Super easy plan to understand. Then the explanation paragraph. How Liberty ID solves the problem. And then you've got a really great visual way of explaining exactly how this program works. Then there's the price options. You've got single plan, couple plan, or the family plan. They even have small business plans if you guys want to contact them. We don't get any money from Liberty ID. We just love all of our clients. And then, oh, also, you would want right here. But don't other insurance companies cover this. And they say insurance companies don't cover identity theft. Liberty ID covers everything that might happen to you. So you've got to get that in there because you want to sort of preemptively offset any sort of pushback that you're going to get. And you're going to need to kind of intuit what is it that customers are going to push back and where. So there's your wireframe, this website. You can check those things off the list. Liberty did a great job with that. And then our restore, replace, and resolve guarantee. Once again, hitting that note of like we get everything back. And you've got some visuals there. Then another cost. It's going to take 200 plus hours on your own versus 1 and 1 1⁄2 hours with Liberty ID. And so they spell that out for you in terms of getting all your stuff back. Another explanatory paragraph. And then, of course, get covered. Questions call us. The phone number is there. If your clients want their phone number there, that's important too. This is a little tool that they put in that you can type in your email address and see if you've already been attacked or you've already been compromised. And almost everybody has. If you were ever on Myspace, you were compromised. They have your information already. They just haven't gone in and attacked you yet because they're doing that at random. Then the junk drawer at the bottom. Everything else that most companies want to put at the top of the website, we put at the bottom. This is an effective website. I talked to Travis the other day. It's working great for them. They're seeing their business increase because they're communicating clearly. But here's another example of a website that we wire framed on paper. And they actually, not even in-house here, in their design, their designers actually worked to create a beautiful website. This one is working great. Another example of what's possible when you get your text straight and you obey the Storybrand framework.
